contact.p_1: Contact the Pisum team for demos, support, partnerships, or clinic pricing. We're your dedicated radiology software provider — every message is read personally and answered within 24 hours.
contact.card1_desc: For technical support, billing questions, and general inquiries. We read every message personally and aim to resolve issues quickly.
contact.card2_desc: Deploying PISUM across a radiology department? Let's talk. We offer volume pricing, onboarding support, and custom configurations for clinics and hospitals.
contact.card3_desc: Found a bug or have a feature request? We want to know. Every report is reviewed by our engineering team and logged in our internal tracker.
contact.p_2: Have a question? Check the FAQ first — it's probably answered there.
contact.button_2: Ask AI 1
contact.piw_msg:
Hi! I'm PISUM AI 👋
Ask me anything about features, pricing, setup, or templates. I'll give you a straight answer.

documentation.p_1: Step-by-step guides on radiology report templates, AI voice dictation setup, and how to automate radiology reports with Pisum.
documentation.inst_p1: Pisum is a lightweight desktop application designed for Windows. It requires zero complex IT configuration.
documentation.inst_li1: Download the latest installer using the link provided in your welcome email.
documentation.inst_li3: Follow the on-screen instructions. Administrator privileges are generally not required.
documentation.inst_note_body: Pisum operates completely offline by default. Patient data is never uploaded to any cloud server.
documentation.tpl_p1: Pisum comes pre-loaded with 112 expert templates covering 5 modalities (CT, MRI, Ultrasound, X-Ray, Interventional).
documentation.tpl_p2: On the left panel, navigate through the modality folders (e.g., CT > Abdomen-Pelvis) or use the search bar at the top to quickly find a specific protocol.
documentation.tpl_note_body: You can edit any built-in template or create your own by clicking the 'New Template' button. These are saved locally to your profile.
documentation.dict_p1: The built-in Sally AI engine allows you to dictate reports hands-free in 23 languages, automatically correcting radiology-specific terms.
documentation.dict_li1: Click the 🎙️ icon or press F4 to start/stop recording.
documentation.dict_li2: Select your preferred language from the dropdown menu in the dictation toolbar.
documentation.exp_p1: Pisum is designed to act as a companion tool that easily feeds your existing systems without complex integrations.
documentation.exp_p2: Click the 'Copy Report' button (or Ctrl+C) once your report is finished. The text formatting is preserved and ready to be pasted directly into your RIS or PACS text editor.
documentation.exp_p3: Use the 'Export to DOCX' button to save a professionally formatted document, ideal for private clinics handing reports directly to patients.
documentation.trans_p1: The Report Translation feature lets you convert any completed radiology report into another language in one click. Clinical terminology is preserved and the report structure remains intact — available on Pro, Expert, and Clinic plans.
documentation.trans_li1: Open a completed report from your worklist.
documentation.trans_li2: Click Translate Report in the toolbar (or use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+T).
documentation.trans_li3: Select the target language from the dropdown (23 languages supported).
documentation.trans_li4: Click Translate. The translated report opens in a side-by-side view within seconds.
documentation.trans_li5: Review, then export the translated version to PDF or Word as usual.
documentation.trans_note1_body: The original report is never overwritten. Translation creates a separate copy linked to the same patient exam — both versions appear in the worklist.
documentation.trans_p2: All 23 PISUM interface languages are available as translation targets, including French, Spanish, German, Arabic, Portuguese, Italian, Dutch, Polish, and more.
documentation.trans_note2_body: Pro: 100 translations/month · Expert: Unlimited · Clinic: Unlimited. Translation requires an active internet connection.
documentation.lan_p1: Available on Expert and Clinic plans. Share the patient database across multiple workstations in your clinic — no internet required, no cloud storage, no extra server to set up.
documentation.lan_p2: All workstations point to a single SQLite database file stored on a shared network folder (NAS, Windows SMB share, or mapped drive). PISUM handles concurrent access automatically using WAL locking. Patient data is encrypted end-to-end with AES-256-GCM before it ever touches the network.
documentation.lan_prereq1: A shared network folder accessible by all workstations in read/write (NAS, Windows SMB share, or mapped drive like Z:\)
documentation.lan_prereq2: SMB 3.0 or higher recommended for stable file locking
documentation.lan_prereq3: All PCs must be on the same LAN or VPN
documentation.lan_setup1: On your server or NAS, create a shared folder — e.g. \\SERVER\PISUM — and give read/write access to all workstations.
documentation.lan_setup2: Open PISUM on the first workstation → go to Settings → Local Network (LAN).
documentation.lan_setup3: Fill in the Network Path (e.g. \\SERVER\PISUM or Z:\PISUM), a unique Workstation ID (e.g. WORKSTATION-01), and your Clinic Name.
documentation.lan_setup4: Enter a network password (minimum 6 characters). You will use the exact same password on every workstation.
documentation.lan_setup5: Click Activate. The badge turns ● Connected and the shared database is created automatically on the network folder.
documentation.lan_setup6: Repeat steps 2–5 on every other workstation — same path, same password, different Workstation ID.
documentation.lan_note_body: If you see "Max workstations reached", either upgrade to Clinic (unlimited) or open the .workstations.json file in your shared folder and remove the entry for the stale workstation.
documentation.lan_sec1: All patient data is encrypted with AES-256-GCM before being written to the shared folder — the raw database file is unreadable without the key.
documentation.lan_sec2: The encryption key (.enc_network_key) is stored in the shared folder and is itself protected by your network password (PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256, 600 000 iterations).
documentation.lan_sec3: The network password is never saved to disk — it is only held in memory for the duration of the session.
documentation.lan_sec4: Every access, modification, and export is logged in a GDPR-compliant audit trail with timestamp and workstation ID.
documentation.lan_trouble1: Badge stays "Disconnected" — verify the shared folder path is correct and accessible from this PC (try opening it in Windows Explorer first).
documentation.lan_trouble2: "Wrong password" error — the network password must be identical on all workstations. Re-enter it carefully; it is case-sensitive.
documentation.lan_trouble3: Slow or locked database — ensure your NAS or share supports SMB 3.0. Some older NAS firmware versions do not support SQLite WAL locking correctly; update the firmware or switch to a Windows-shared folder.
documentation.supp_p1: If you encounter any issues not covered in this documentation, our team is here to help.
documentation.button_2: Ask AI 1
documentation.piw_msg:
Hi! I'm PISUM AI 👋
Ask me anything about features, pricing, setup, or templates. I'll give you a straight answer.

dpia.p_1: Formal DPIA for PISUM radiology reporting software. Covers all processing activities involving personal and special-category health data.
dpia.s1p1: PISUM is a Windows desktop software for radiology reporting used by radiologists, technicians, and healthcare secretaries. It processes personal data in the context of medical imaging — a field that involves special-category health data under GDPR Art. 9.
dpia.s1p2: A DPIA is required under GDPR Art. 35(3) when processing is "likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons." The following criteria from EDPB Guidelines 4/2019 apply to PISUM:
dpia.s1l1: Special category data (Art. 9) — voice audio streams may include dictated medical information about patients.
dpia.s1l2: Large-scale processing — PISUM targets clinics and hospitals processing hundreds to thousands of reports per month.
dpia.s1l3: Innovative technology — AI voice dictation (Deepgram) and AI text enhancement (Gemini) are newly deployed in a medical context.
dpia.s1l4: Third-country transfer — voice audio is transferred to Deepgram (USA) under Art. 49(1)(a).
dpia.ib1: Legal basis for this DPIA. GDPR Art. 35(1): "Where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment."
dpia.s1p3: This DPIA covers all three processing activities (PA) identified in PISUM v2.x:
dpia.s3p1: PISUM uses Supabase (PostgreSQL, hosted in eu-west-1 / Ireland) to manage user accounts, authentication tokens, license keys, and subscription status. Data stored: full name, professional email, hashed password (managed by Supabase Auth), license key, OS version, subscription tier, and last login timestamp.
dpia.s3p2: GDPR Art. 6(1)(b) — processing necessary for performance of a contract (software license agreement).
dpia.pa1_m1: Row-level security (RLS), JWT auth, EU hosting
dpia.pa1_m2: Supabase rate limiting, email confirmation, Fernet-encrypted local token storage
dpia.pa1_m3: SCC 2021/914 in place; Supabase notifies controller within 72h per DPA
dpia.s3l1: Supabase hosted exclusively in EU (eu-west-1 / Ireland) — no third-country transfer required.
dpia.s3l2: Row-Level Security (RLS) policies: each user can only access their own profile row.
dpia.s3l3: Local auth tokens encrypted with Fernet (AES-128-CBC + HMAC-SHA256) in APPDATA.
dpia.s3l4: Supabase has signed Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC 2021/914) — DPA in place.
dpia.s3l5: Retention: account data deleted within 30 days of subscription termination upon request.
dpia.ib3: Residual risk: LOW. No health data is processed in PA-1. Supabase EU hosting eliminates cross-border transfer risk. RLS and token encryption reduce unauthorized access risk to acceptable levels.
dpia.s4p1: When voice dictation is activated, PISUM streams real-time audio from the user's microphone to Deepgram, Inc. (San Francisco, CA, USA) via WebSocket Secure (WSS/TLS 1.3). Deepgram processes the audio using the Nova-2 Medical model and returns a text transcription. The audio stream is not stored by Deepgram after transcription. No patient identifiers are included in the audio unless explicitly dictated by the user.
dpia.s4p2: GDPR Art. 49(1)(a) — explicit consent for third-country transfer. A dedicated consent dialog discloses all risks (including the Deepgram benchmarking risk) before the first activation. Consent is stored locally with timestamp and version.
dpia.ib4:
⚠ Identified high risk: Deepgram benchmarking sharing. Deepgram's data processing policy permits sharing audio recordings with "industry peers" (competitor companies) for model benchmarking and evaluation purposes. Medical audio may include terminology that, in context, is health-adjacent. This represents a material risk that audio could be accessed by third parties outside the DPA relationship.

Status: Risk disclosed in consent dialog (v1.1) and in this DPIA. PISUM is seeking a DPA with Deepgram (security@deepgram.com) that explicitly restricts processing to core service delivery and prohibits benchmarking sharing. Until DPA is signed, users must be warned and must actively consent.
dpia.pa2_m1: WSS/TLS 1.3 encryption for all audio streams
dpia.pa2_m2: Deepgram policy: immediate deletion post-transcription. Audit via DPA.
dpia.pa2_m3: Explicit consent disclosure; DPA negotiation in progress; manual entry alternative provided
dpia.pa2_m4: In-app warning; consent dialog explicitly warns users not to dictate identifiers; SCC in place
dpia.pa2_m5: SCC 2021/914 + explicit consent (Art. 49(1)(a)); Deepgram deletes audio immediately
dpia.s4l1: Voice dictation disabled by default. Cannot be activated without explicit Art. 49(1)(a) consent.
dpia.s4l2: Consent dialog (v1.1) fully discloses: Deepgram (USA), SCC, benchmarking risk, right to withdraw, manual alternative.
dpia.s4l3: Consent can be withdrawn at any time from Settings → Privacy, without affecting prior processing.
dpia.s4l4: Audio streamed over WSS/TLS 1.3 — encrypted in transit.
dpia.s4l5: Deepgram Nova-2 Medical model: audio deleted immediately after transcription per Deepgram policy.
dpia.s4l6: Manual text entry available as a zero-transfer alternative.
dpia.s4l7: DPA negotiation pending with Deepgram (security@deepgram.com) to restrict benchmarking sharing.
dpia.ib4b: Residual risk: MEDIUM (pending DPA). The benchmarking sharing risk remains MEDIUM until a DPA is signed with Deepgram that explicitly prohibits sharing with third parties for benchmarking. Once signed, residual risk drops to LOW. Current mitigation: explicit user consent with full risk disclosure.
dpia.s5p1: PISUM stores radiology reports, patient metadata (name, date of birth, patient ID, study date, modality), and report history in a local SQLite database on the user's workstation. This database constitutes special-category health data under GDPR Art. 9. The institution (clinic/hospital) is the Data Controller for this processing; PISUM acts as a tool (data processor in the local context).
dpia.s5p2: GDPR Art. 9(2)(h) — processing necessary for the provision of health care by healthcare professionals subject to professional secrecy obligations.
dpia.pa3_m1: AES-256-GCM database encryption; Windows login required; role-based access control
dpia.pa3_m2: No network exposure; database never leaves local filesystem
dpia.pa3_m3: Role-based access (admin/radiologist/technician/secretary); audit log for all accesses
dpia.pa3_m4: Export to PDF/DICOM SR; institution responsible for backup (HIPAA §164.308(a)(7)); PISUM does not manage backups
dpia.pa3_m5: SQLite PRAGMA secure_delete=ON; WAL journal cleared on close
dpia.s5l1: AES-256-GCM encryption at rest for the entire SQLite database file.
dpia.s5l2: PRAGMA secure_delete=ON — deleted rows are overwritten with zeros (no data remanence).
dpia.s5l3: WAL mode (Write-Ahead Logging) for crash safety; journal cleared on application close.
dpia.s5l4: Role-based access control enforced at application layer: secretary cannot access all patient records; roles are assigned by admin.
dpia.s5l5: Audit log in encrypted database records all data access events (who, what, when).
dpia.s5l6: Session timeout: session revoked on application exit, requiring re-authentication on next launch.
dpia.s5l7: Foreign keys enabled (PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON) — referential integrity prevents orphaned sensitive records.
dpia.ib5: Residual risk: LOW-MEDIUM. The primary residual risk is physical theft of the workstation. This is mitigated by AES-256-GCM encryption — a stolen disk without the decryption key is unreadable. Backup responsibility lies with the institution per their own data management policy.
dpia.t6_r1c3: EU hosting, RLS, SCC, Fernet tokens
dpia.t6_r2c1: PA-2: Voice dictation (Deepgram USA) — transit & storage
dpia.t6_r2c3: WSS/TLS 1.3, immediate audio deletion, explicit consent
dpia.t6_r3c3: Full risk disclosure in consent dialog; manual alternative
dpia.s7p1: After implementation of all identified mitigations, the overall residual risk profile of PISUM v2.x is assessed as MEDIUM — driven entirely by the Deepgram benchmarking risk (PA-2) which will be reduced to LOW once a DPA is signed with Deepgram.
dpia.s7l1: [Priority: HIGH] Sign DPA with Deepgram Inc. (contact: security@deepgram.com) — restricting processing to core service delivery and prohibiting benchmarking sharing. Target: within 60 days of this DPIA.
dpia.s7l2: [Priority: MEDIUM] Implement in-app warning when user dictates content that appears to include patient name or ID formats (regex heuristic).
dpia.s7l3: [Priority: LOW] Evaluate feasibility of on-device STT (Whisper.cpp) as a Deepgram alternative for users with strict data sovereignty requirements.
dpia.s7l5: Upon any significant change to processing activities (new sub-processor, new data category, new feature using personal data), or
dpia.s7l6: Following a data breach or security incident affecting personal data.
dpia.ib7: Conclusion: Processing by PISUM v2.x is technically and legally justified under GDPR. All high-risk processing activities are covered by appropriate legal bases (Art. 6, Art. 9, Art. 49(1)(a)) and proportionate technical safeguards. The one outstanding MEDIUM risk (Deepgram benchmarking) is managed by full disclosure and explicit consent, with an ongoing DPA negotiation to eliminate it. PISUM may proceed with processing.
dpia.s8p1: In accordance with GDPR Art. 35(2), the Data Protection Officer has been consulted during the preparation of this DPIA and has provided the following assessment.
dpia.ib8:
Opinion: FAVOURABLE — subject to one condition.
The DPO finds that PISUM v2.x processing is proportionate, lawful, and based on appropriate legal bases. The DPO endorses the mitigations implemented for PA-1 and PA-3. For PA-2, the DPO endorses proceeding with explicit consent under Art. 49(1)(a) as an interim measure, and requires that a DPA with Deepgram be signed within 90 days of this DPIA date. The DPO will re-evaluate PA-2 upon receipt of the signed DPA.
dpia.s8p2: For questions about this DPIA, contact the DPO at support@pisum.app with subject line [DPIA]. The full privacy policy is available at pisum.app/rgpd.html. The DPO designation is available at pisum.app/dpo.html.

dpo.p_1: PISUM has formally designated a Data Protection Officer in accordance with GDPR Art. 37. The DPO is your independent point of contact for all privacy and data rights matters.
dpo.dpo_title: Designated DPO · GDPR Art. 37 · Since May 2026
dpo.s1p1: In accordance with GDPR Art. 37(1)(c), PISUM has designated a Data Protection Officer. The designation is mandatory because PISUM's core activities involve large-scale processing of special-category data (GDPR Art. 9) — specifically, radiology-related health data and voice audio streams that may include health-adjacent content.
dpo.s1h3: Why a DPO is mandatory for PISUM
dpo.t1_r1c1: Large-scale processing of special category data
dpo.t1_r1c3: Yes — health data in reports; voice audio dictation
dpo.t1_r2c3: Yes — subscription management, AI features monitoring
dpo.t1_r3c1: Processing involving sensitive categories at scale
dpo.ib1: Designation scope. The DPO is designated for all processing activities conducted by PISUM as data controller, including: account management (Supabase), voice dictation (Deepgram), and the local patient database tool (SQLite). The DPO is also available to institutional users (clinics, hospitals) who use PISUM and have questions about their own compliance.
dpo.s2p1: The PISUM DPO performs all tasks mandated by GDPR Art. 39:
dpo.s2p2: The DPO informs and advises PISUM and its employees of their obligations under GDPR and applicable national data protection law. This includes advising on new features before release, reviewing consent dialogs, and assessing sub-processor agreements.
dpo.s2p3: The DPO monitors PISUM's compliance with GDPR, including the assignment of responsibilities, raising awareness, training of staff, and the related audits. Monitoring activities include:
dpo.s2l1: Annual review of all consent texts and privacy notices.
dpo.s2l3: Monitoring the incident log for events requiring Art. 33 notification.
dpo.s2l4: Reviewing DPIA annually and on any significant change.
dpo.s2p4: The DPO provides advice and monitors the Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) required by Art. 35 for PISUM's high-risk processing activities. The DPO reviewed and endorsed the PISUM DPIA v1.0 (May 2026) — see pisum.app/dpia.html.
dpo.s2p5: The DPO acts as the primary contact point for the supervisory authority (CNIL for France; INPDP for Tunisia) on all processing matters. In the event of a data breach, the DPO coordinates the Art. 33 notification within 72 hours.
dpo.s2p6: The DPO receives, processes, and responds to data subject requests (access, erasure, portability, objection, rectification) within the 30-day deadline specified by GDPR Art. 12. The DPO maintains a register of all requests received.
dpo.s3p1: GDPR Art. 38 requires that the DPO acts independently and is not dismissed or penalized for performing their tasks. PISUM guarantees the following:
dpo.s3l1: No conflict of interest: The DPO does not hold any role within PISUM that could conflict with data protection responsibilities (Art. 38(6)).
dpo.s3l2: Reporting directly to management: The DPO reports directly to the highest management level (Art. 38(3)) and is not subject to instructions regarding the performance of their tasks.
dpo.s3l3: Adequate resources: The DPO is provided with sufficient time, resources, and access to personal data and processing operations to maintain expert knowledge (Art. 38(2)).
dpo.s3l4: Confidentiality: The DPO is bound by secrecy and confidentiality regarding the performance of tasks (Art. 38(5)).
dpo.s3l5: No dismissal for performance of tasks: The DPO shall not be dismissed or penalized for performing their data protection tasks (Art. 38(3)).
dpo.ib3: Independence in practice. The DPO reviewed and approved the Deepgram benchmarking risk disclosure in PISUM v2.x — including the requirement to obtain explicit consent before voice dictation and the mandate to sign a DPA with Deepgram within 90 days. This decision was made independently of commercial considerations.
dpo.s4h: Contact the DPO & exercising your rights
dpo.s4p1: Any user, patient, or third party may contact the PISUM DPO directly to:
dpo.s4l1: Exercise data subject rights (access, erasure, portability, rectification, objection)
dpo.s4l2: Ask questions about PISUM's data processing practices
dpo.s4l3: Report a suspected data protection violation
dpo.s4l4: Request a copy of the DPIA or sub-processor list
dpo.s4l5: Lodge a complaint about how PISUM handles personal data
dpo.t2_r1c3: Maximum 30 days (GDPR Art. 12). Complex requests: 60 days with notice.
dpo.t2_r2c3: Within 5 business days for general inquiries
dpo.t2_r3c3: DPO acknowledges within 24 hours; Art. 33 notification within 72 hours
dpo.ib4:
Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. If you believe your rights under GDPR have not been respected, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority in your country of habitual residence, place of work, or place of the alleged infringement — regardless of contacting the DPO first.

— France: CNIL — www.cnil.fr
— Tunisia: INPDP — www.inpdp.nat.tn
— Algeria: Commission nationale de protection des données personnelles (CNPDP)
— Morocco: Commission Nationale de contrôle de la protection des Données à caractère Personnel (CNDP)
dpo.s5p1: GDPR Art. 37(7) requires the controller to publish the contact details of the DPO and communicate them to the supervisory authority. PISUM has notified the relevant supervisory authority of the DPO designation as follows:
dpo.ib5: DPO register. The DPO maintains a register of all processing activities (Art. 30 record of processing activities / ROPA) and all data subject requests. This register is available to the supervisory authority upon request.
dpo.s6p1: By this formal designation act, PISUM hereby designates the Data Protection Officer identified below, in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) Art. 37(1)(c).
dpo.s6p2: The designated DPO accepts this designation and acknowledges the tasks defined in GDPR Art. 39, the independence guarantee of Art. 38, and the obligation of confidentiality under Art. 38(5).
dpo.s6p3: This designation is effective immediately and shall remain in force until revoked in writing by PISUM management or the DPO. Any change in DPO shall be communicated to the relevant supervisory authority without undue delay.
dpo.s6p4: The DPO's contact details are published at pisum.app/dpo.html in compliance with Art. 37(7).
dpo.s6l1: Privacy Policy — full data processing disclosure (GDPR Art. 13)
dpo.s6l2: DPIA — Data Protection Impact Assessment (GDPR Art. 35)
dpo.s6l3: Terms of Service — software license agreement

faq.h1_1:
Radiology dictation software
— your questions answered.
faq.p_1: Everything you need to know about PISUM. Can't find what you're looking for? Reach our team directly.
faq.no_results: No results found for your search.
faq.button_7: What is radiology dictation software?
faq.ans_1:
Radiology dictation software is a specialized voice recognition tool designed specifically for radiologists. Unlike generic speech-to-text tools, it understands radiology-specific medical terminology, imaging modalities (CT, MRI, X-Ray, Ultrasound, PET-CT), anatomical nomenclature, and clinical phrasing.

PISUM's built-in radiology dictation engine — Sally AI — goes a step further: it processes audio in real time via a secure cloud API (audio is never stored, only the text transcription is returned), supports 23 languages with radiology-specific medical vocabulary, and dictates directly into structured report fields. The result is a formatted, exportable report — no post-editing, no per-minute voice fees. Patient reports always stay on your local machine.
faq.button_8: Is AI good for radiology reports?
faq.ans_2:
Yes — when used correctly as a productivity layer, not a replacement for clinical judgment. AI in radiology reporting can dramatically reduce the time spent writing repetitive boilerplate, eliminate copy-paste errors across reports, enforce consistent medical terminology, and significantly reduce radiologist burnout.

Research shows radiologists spend up to 40% of their shift on report writing. AI-assisted radiology workflow software like PISUM can reduce this by 60–80%, bringing average report time to under 60 seconds. PISUM's approach: AI structures and transcribes, the radiologist validates and signs. AI features use a secure cloud API — audio is never stored, and patient reports never leave your local machine. The radiologist stays in control; the AI removes the friction.
faq.button_9: What is PISUM and who is it for?
faq.ans_3: PISUM is a radiology reporting desktop application designed for radiologists and radiology departments. It provides 112 structured report templates, AI-assisted voice dictation, multi-language support, and one-click PDF/Word export — running on your Windows workstation with cloud-powered AI features.
faq.button_10: Does PISUM require an internet connection?
faq.ans_4: Yes. PISUM requires an active internet connection for AI Dictation, AI Enhancer, and subscription management. Your reports and templates are stored locally on your machine, but AI features connect securely to our cloud API. Internet is always required for full functionality.
faq.button_11: What operating systems are supported?
faq.ans_5: PISUM supports Windows 10/11 (64-bit). macOS and Linux versions are currently in development. A minimum of 8 GB RAM is recommended; 16 GB is advised for optimal voice dictation performance.
faq.button_12: Can multiple radiologists use PISUM on the same site?
faq.ans_6: Yes — with the Clinic plan. It includes 5 users, multi-site management, an admin dashboard, and LAN network sync across workstations. Additional seats can be added from the dashboard at €69/extra user.
faq.button_13: How does the voice dictation (Sally AI) work?
faq.ans_7: Sally AI is PISUM's voice recognition engine. It converts your speech into structured radiology report text in real time via a secure cloud API — audio is never stored. It is optimized for medical vocabulary across 23 supported languages.
faq.button_14: How many templates are available? Can I create my own?
faq.ans_8: PISUM ships with 112 structured templates covering all major imaging modalities (CT, MRI, X-Ray, Ultrasound, PET-CT…). You can also create and save unlimited custom templates — either from scratch or by modifying existing ones. Custom templates are stored locally and travel with your user profile.
faq.button_15: What export formats are supported?
faq.ans_9: PISUM exports to PDF and Microsoft Word (.docx). Reports can be formatted with custom headers, footers, logos, and signatures. Bulk export for batch reporting is available on the Clinic plan. Direct RIS/PACS integration is on our roadmap.
faq.button_16: Can I use PISUM in languages other than English?
faq.ans_10: Yes. The interface and report templates are available in 23 languages including French, German, Spanish, Arabic, Italian, Portuguese, and more. Voice dictation is also language-specific — Sally AI understands medical terminology in each supported language.
faq.button_17: Can I translate a completed report to another language? NEW
faq.ans_11: Yes — this is a new feature in PISUM v2.9.8. The Report Translation tool lets you convert any completed radiology report into one of 23 languages in seconds. Clinical terminology is preserved and the report structure stays intact. The original is never modified — a separate translated copy is created. Available on Pro (100/month), Expert (unlimited), and Clinic (unlimited) plans. Requires an internet connection.
faq.button_18: Can I brand reports with my clinic's identity?
faq.ans_12: Yes — the Clinic plan includes full custom branding. Upload your institution's logo, customize headers, footers, color accents, and digital signature fields. Every exported report will carry your department's visual identity.
faq.button_19: What's the difference between Pro and Clinic?
faq.ans_13:
PISUM has 5 plans:

Free (€0) — 10 templates, 2 languages, PDF export only, 50 reports/month, 30 min/mo AI dictation.

Starter (€29/mo) — 20 templates, 23 languages, PDF + Word, 500 min/mo AI dictation, unlimited reports.

Pro (€79/mo) — 112+ templates, 23 languages, 2,000 min/mo AI dictation, full worklist, priority support. Most popular.

Expert (€129/mo) — everything in Pro + unlimited AI dictation, LAN sync (1 site, 3 PCs), full statistics, chat support.

Clinic (€399/mo) — everything in Expert + 5 users included, multi-site LAN sync, unlimited workstations, dedicated onboarding.
faq.button_20: Can I switch plans anytime?
faq.ans_14: Yes, anytime. Upgrades take effect immediately and are prorated to your current billing cycle. Downgrades apply at the start of the next cycle — you keep your current features until then.
faq.button_21: Is there a discount for annual billing?
faq.ans_15: Yes — 20% off when you pay annually. Examples: Starter €29→€23/mo (saves €72/year), Pro €79→€63/mo (saves €192/year), Expert €129→€103/mo (saves €312/year), Clinic €399→€319/mo (saves €960/year). Annual billing is available on all paid plans at checkout.
faq.button_22: Is there a free trial?
faq.ans_16: Yes. All paid plans include a 14-day free trial — no credit card required. There is also a permanent Free plan with limited features you can use indefinitely. A 30-day money-back guarantee applies to all paid plans.
faq.button_23: Is PISUM GDPR / RGPD compliant?
faq.ans_17: Yes. All patient data is generated and stored locally on your machine. No patient data is ever transmitted to our servers. PISUM only collects anonymized license telemetry (activation key, OS version). See our Privacy Policy for the full compliance overview.
faq.button_24: Where is patient data stored?
faq.ans_18: Patient reports are stored locally on your machine. PISUM never uploads patient data to any server. Account data (name, email, subscription status) is hosted on Supabase with EU servers. Local backups and RIS/PACS archiving remain your institution's responsibility.
faq.button_25: Can PISUM be used in hospitals with strict IT security policies?
faq.ans_19: Yes. Patient reports never leave your machine, which satisfies most healthcare data-handling requirements. PISUM requires outbound internet access for AI features and subscription sync. Contact us for enterprise deployment documentation and firewall requirements.
faq.p_2: Our team is happy to help with anything not covered above.
faq.button_26: Ask AI 1
faq.piw_msg:
Hi! I'm PISUM AI 👋
Ask me anything about features, pricing, setup, or templates. I'll give you a straight answer.

index.p_5: See how radiologists generate structured, AI-enhanced reports in under a minute — from dictation to final PDF.
index.why_p1:
The major platforms were built for hospital IT departments.
PISUM was built for the radiologist managing their own practice.
index.why_pillar2_desc: Patient data never leaves your machine
index.why_p2: Dragon Medical and PowerScribe were designed for large hospital IT teams — cloud-dependent, enterprise-priced, built around procurement cycles rather than clinical workflows.
index.why_p3: PISUM takes the opposite approach: patient data stays encrypted on your machine, reports follow structured radiology vocabulary, and the whole workflow is built for GDPR and HIPAA compliance from the start.
index.p_9: Radiologists spend up to 40% of their time typing repetitive boilerplate — time that belongs with patients.
index.p_10: Yesterday's report pasted into today's. Errors introduced. Patient data mixed. Compliance risk grows.
index.p_11: Reporting in a second language takes twice as long and increases terminology inconsistencies.
index.p_12: Expert-structured templates with guided fields. Fill in findings. Export. Done. No reformatting ever.
index.p_13: Standardized terminology, required fields enforced, patient data isolated per report. Zero copy-paste.
index.p_14: Switch language per report. PISUM handles structure and clinical terminology natively in each.
index.p_15: Guided structured fields complete your report as you type. Average completion: 47 seconds. No learning curve.
index.p_16: Report natively in English, French, Spanish, German, Italian, Portuguese, and 17 more — terminology validated by native radiologists.
index.p_17: Expert-built for CT, MRI, X-Ray, and Ultrasound across every body system. Each field has clinical validation.
index.p_18: Professionally formatted output ready for PACS, EMR, or direct delivery. Your letterhead, your layout.
index.p_19: Compatible with all major voice recognition tools. Dictate directly into structured fields.
index.p_20: Manage multiple radiologists from one admin panel. Custom branding per site. Centralized reporting dashboard.
index.p_21: Share the patient database across all workstations in your clinic via a shared local network folder. One shared folder on your LAN is all it takes. AES-256 encrypted end-to-end.
index.p_22: Full PACS-style worklist with accession numbers, exam status, and GDPR-compliant audit trail. Every access, modification, and export is logged with timestamp and user.
index.p_23: Instantly translate any completed radiology report into another language. Clinical terminology preserved, report structure intact — one click, 23 target languages.
index.p_24: Designed for reading rooms. Near-black surface palette, teal accents, zero eye strain during long reporting sessions. Your eyes will thank you after shift 3.
index.p_25: Click a feature to see how it works — no signup needed.

hero.title: Radiology reporting software
hero.tagline: Dictate naturally. Let AI enhance your reports.

rgpd.p_1: 100% local architecture · Zero cloud · GDPR Art. 25 · HDS-Ready
rgpd.b1d: Reports and patient data never leave your machine. No cloud access to medical data.
rgpd.b2d: No cloud host, analytics service, or third party accesses your patients' health data.
rgpd.b3d: Sally AI processes audio via PISUM's secure cloud API. Audio is never stored.
rgpd.s1p1: PISUM is designed according to the principle of Privacy by Design (GDPR Art. 25). The software distinguishes two fundamentally separate categories of data:
rgpd.ib1: ✓ Health data — ZERO collection. Radiology reports, patient data (name, ID, date of birth), medical images, and voice transcriptions are never transmitted to PISUM SAS or any third party.
rgpd.s2p1: Radiology reports, patient data, and medical images never leave your machine. PISUM does not require an Internet connection for data entry and report export. Only AI features (Dictation, Enhancer) require network access to the secure PISUM API.
rgpd.s2h2: 🎙️ AI Voice Dictation — secure cloud API
rgpd.s2p2: Sally AI voice dictation processes audio in real time via PISUM's secure API. Audio is never stored — only the text transcription is sent back to your local machine. No identifiable patient data is included in the audio streams sent to the API.
rgpd.ib2: HDS Compliance. Medical data remains on your machine. PISUM's AI API only processes dictation audio, never patient data.
rgpd.s3p1: Since the software runs isolated on the user's workstation, no third-party sub-processor (Cloud host, analytics service) has access to medical data. Legal archiving and secure storage are the sole responsibility of the institution's RIS/PACS system, ensuring full data sovereignty.
rgpd.s3p2: To ensure software stability, PISUM generates local technical error logs. These files are strictly programmed to capture no business data:
rgpd.li1: No patient names, dates of birth, or identification numbers (ID).
rgpd.li2: No medical terms, report content, or voice transcriptions.
rgpd.li3: Only system error codes, loading times, and click events.
rgpd.s4p1: The GDPR guarantees patients the right of access, rectification, erasure, and portability of their data. Given that PISUM retains no data after the application is closed, it is technically impossible to query or delete a patient file within it.
rgpd.ib3: Exercising patient rights. The exercise of these rights must be directed to the Data Controller (the DPO of the clinic or hospital) who will intervene directly on the RIS/PACS or hospital information system (HIS) where the final reports are archived.
rgpd.s5p1: For any questions regarding data protection or to exercise your rights as a user of the PISUM service:
rgpd.piw_msg:
Hi! I'm PISUM AI 👋
Ask me anything about features, pricing, setup, or templates. I'll give you a straight answer.

terms.p_1: By using PISUM, you agree to these terms. Please read them carefully.
terms.s1p1: These Terms of Use govern access to and use of the PISUM radiology reporting software, developed by PISUM SAS.
terms.s1p2: By installing, activating, or using PISUM, you acknowledge that you have read and accept these terms in full.
terms.ib1: Professional use only. PISUM is exclusively intended for qualified healthcare professionals (radiologists, specialist physicians). Use by unqualified persons is expressly prohibited.
terms.s2p1: PISUM SAS grants the user a personal, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to use the software for professional radiology activities.
terms.s2l1: Install and use PISUM on the number of workstations included in your plan.
terms.s2l2: Create radiology reports for professional use.
terms.s2l3: Customize included templates for your medical practice.
terms.s2l4: You may not redistribute, sublicense, or sell access to the software.
terms.s2l5: You may not decompile, disassemble, or attempt to extract the source code.
terms.s2l6: You may not use PISUM to develop a competing product.
terms.s3p1: PISUM is a report drafting aid. Generated reports remain under the exclusive responsibility of the radiologist who signs and validates them.
terms.ib3: ⚠️ Important disclaimer. The user remains fully responsible for the medical accuracy and regulatory compliance of each report issued via PISUM. PISUM SAS declines all liability for diagnostic errors.
terms.s3p2: Provided templates are structural models to be adapted to each patient's specific clinical context by the healthcare professional.
terms.s4p1: Patient data and reports are stored exclusively on your local machine. No patient data, report content, or medical image is transmitted to PISUM SAS servers.
terms.s4p2: The only data collected concerns account and license management. See our GDPR Privacy Policy.
terms.s4l1: Account data is hosted in the European Union.
terms.s4l2: Patient data stays on your local machine — we have no access to it.
terms.s4l3: Voice dictation (Sally AI) is processed via a secure cloud API in real time — audio is never stored.
terms.s5p1: PISUM, its source code, 112 templates, Sally AI dictation engine, and all components are the exclusive property of PISUM SAS.
terms.s5p2: Reports generated by the user belong entirely to them. PISUM SAS claims no rights over the medical content produced.
terms.s6p1: You may cancel your subscription at any time from your account dashboard. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period.
terms.s6p2: PISUM SAS reserves the right to suspend your access immediately in case of terms violation or misuse.
terms.s7p1: To the extent permitted by applicable law, PISUM SAS shall not be liable for indirect or consequential damages arising from use of the software.
terms.s7p2: PISUM SAS's total liability shall not exceed the amount paid by the user over the last 12 months.
terms.ib7: Service availability. As a local application, PISUM's availability does not depend on our servers. We guarantee the licensing service with a 99.5% uptime objective.
terms.s8p1: These terms are governed by French law. In case of persistent dispute, the courts of Paris shall have exclusive jurisdiction.
terms.s8p2: For any question regarding these terms: support@pisum.app
